description: Logic+, Vogue Gen2, and Evomax 2 ranges — combi and system boilers common across UK housing and light commercial.

faultCodes.title: No central heating flow
faultCodes.summary: Pump is running but no flow is being detected through the primary circuit.
quickChecks: Bleed the auto air vent on top of the pump
Free a seized pump at the centre screw
Confirm both flow and return isolation valves fully open

faultCodes.title: Ignition lockout
faultCodes.summary: Boiler has tried the full ignition sequence and given up.
quickChecks: Check gas at other appliances
Clean and gap the ignition electrode
Drain the condensate trap
Measure inlet gas pressure under load
Ensure the condensate pipe is not frozen (very common in winter — pour warm water over the external pipe)

faultCodes.title: Five unsuccessful ignition attempts
faultCodes.summary: Boiler has hit the retry limit on ignition.
likelyCauses: Same causes as L2 — gas supply, ignition, flame-sense, gas valve
quickChecks: Work through the no-ignition flowchart

faultCodes.title: False flame detection
faultCodes.summary: Flame signal present when it should not be.
quickChecks: Dry and inspect the ignition lead and electrode
Soap-test burner with valve closed

faultCodes.title: Limit thermostat operation
faultCodes.summary: Overheat stat has opened.
quickChecks: Check pump and system vented
Inspect heat exchanger for sludge
Measure overheat stat continuity cold

faultCodes.title: Low system pressure
faultCodes.summary: Pressure sensor below operating threshold.
quickChecks: Top up to 1.2 bar cold
Walk the system for leaks
Check PRV discharge outside

faultCodes.title: Flame loss
faultCodes.summary: Flame extinguished mid-run.
quickChecks: Measure gas working pressure under full rate
Inspect flue terminal
Clean flame-sense electrode

faultCodes.title: Fan fault
faultCodes.summary: Fan not achieving or maintaining commanded speed.
quickChecks: Listen to the fan on start-up
Check 230V supply and tacho wiring

faultCodes.title: Flow thermistor fault
faultCodes.summary: Flow NTC open, shorted, or out of range.
likelyCauses: DHW NTC connector corroded from moisture
quickChecks: Re-seat connector
Measure resistance against NTC table
Inspect the NTC connector for green corrosion — clean or replace the connector

faultCodes.title: Return thermistor fault
faultCodes.summary: Return NTC open, shorted, or out of range.
quickChecks: Re-seat connector
Measure resistance against NTC table

faultCodes.title: Outside sensor fault
faultCodes.summary: Optional outside weather compensation sensor reading out of range.
quickChecks: Inspect the external sensor
Check the cable run

faultCodes.title: Low mains voltage
faultCodes.summary: Supply voltage dropped below PCB operating range.
likelyCauses: Shared circuit dropping voltage under load
quickChecks: Measure supply voltage at the fused spur
Check for loose neutral or L connection

faultCodes.title: CH flow switch open
faultCodes.summary: Primary flow switch not seeing flow when pump is running (where fitted).
quickChecks: Flush the flow switch if accessible
Confirm pump operation

faultCodes.title: PCB fault
faultCodes.summary: PCB internal error.
quickChecks: Power-cycle the boiler
Inspect the PCB for moisture damage

faultCodes.title: Communication fault
faultCodes.summary: Communication error between the user interface and the main PCB.
quickChecks: Re-seat the display ribbon cable
Swap in a known-good display

faultCodes.title: BCC / chip card fault
faultCodes.summary: Boiler configuration chip card missing or not being read.
likelyCauses: BCC not inserted after PCB swap
quickChecks: Re-seat the BCC
Clean the contacts
Verify BCC matches boiler rating

faultCodes.title: Ignition failure (Evomax 2)
faultCodes.summary: Commercial lockout — boiler could not establish flame.
quickChecks: Check gas pressure under load
Clean electrode and measure gap
Drain condensate trap

faultCodes.title: Flame loss during operation (Evomax 2)
faultCodes.summary: Flame established but then dropped.
quickChecks: Verify gas working pressure
Inspect flue
Clean flame-sense electrode

faultCodes.title: Fan fault (Evomax 2)
faultCodes.summary: Fan speed not tracking command.
quickChecks: Listen and inspect fan
Check wiring and supply

faultCodes.title: No flame detected after ignition
faultCodes.summary: The boiler established a flame signal on ignition but lost it almost immediately. This differs from L2 where the flame never establishes at all.
likelyCauses: Flame sense electrode dirty or positioned incorrectly
Gas pressure dropping during operation (partial blockage in gas valve)
Electrode lead cracked or shorting to frame
quickChecks: Clean the flame sense electrode with fine emery cloth
Check gas pressure at burner — should hold steady during operation
Inspect electrode lead for cracks, especially near the boot connector
Check gas rate matches the data badge

faultCodes.title: Incorrect air-to-gas ratio detected
faultCodes.summary: The boiler's combustion monitoring has detected the air-to-gas ratio is outside safe limits. This is a safety lockout.
likelyCauses: Flue partially blocked (birds' nest, leaves, or debris)
Condensate trap blocked — backing up into the heat exchanger
Fan speed incorrect — faulty fan or hall effect sensor
Venturi blockage (soot or debris in mixing chamber)
quickChecks: Check flue terminal is clear outside the property
Inspect condensate trap and siphon — clean if blocked
Perform combustion analysis — CO/CO₂ readings will confirm the fault
Inspect the venturi / mixing chamber for soot buildup

faultCodes.title: Loop controller / system wiring fault
faultCodes.summary: The boiler has detected a fault with the external controls wiring or OpenTherm communication. The boiler will still fire but advanced features may not work.
likelyCauses: Room thermostat wiring fault or disconnected
Short circuit on the external controls wiring
quickChecks: Check wiring connections at the boiler terminal strip
If fitted, check the OpenTherm controller is powered and communicating
Try bridging L-N on the room thermostat terminals — if boiler fires, fault is in the wiring
Check for any loose connections on the terminal block

faultCodes.title: DHW temperature too high at outlet
faultCodes.summary: The domestic hot water temperature has exceeded the safe limit at the outlet. This is a safety lockout to prevent scalding.
likelyCauses: DHW thermistor reading incorrectly — measure actual water temperature to confirm
Diverter valve stuck partially open to heating, causing excessive heat
Plate heat exchanger scaled up — uneven heat transfer
PCB not modulating gas valve down fast enough
quickChecks: Run a hot tap and measure actual water temperature with a thermometer
If water isn't actually too hot, suspect faulty DHW NTC — measure its resistance
Check the diverter valve is moving freely to full DHW position
Descale the plate heat exchanger if over 5 years old in hard water area

faultCodes.title: System filling fault / pressure too low
faultCodes.summary: The system pressure has dropped below the minimum required. The boiler may not fire until pressure is topped up.
likelyCauses: Slow leak in the system (radiator valve, pipework joint)
Failed expansion vessel — check pre-charge pressure
Faulty auto air vent weeping water
quickChecks: Check the pressure gauge — top up to 1.0-1.5 bar via the filling loop
Look for drips around radiator valves, joints, and connections
Check the expansion vessel charge pressure via the Schrader valve (should be ~0.75 bar cold)
If pressure drops repeatedly, systematic leak search is needed
